channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Global geopolitical news headlines curated from Tier 1 and Tier 2 sources

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine becomes first country to scale remotely operated interceptor drones
item.description: Ukraine has become the first country to scale the use of remotely operated interceptor drones capable of long-distance strikes, Defense Minister Mykhailo Fedorov said. The systems are already being used to destroy aerial targets in combat.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine expands ePoints system rewarding battlefield kills with weapons credits
item.description: Ukraine is developing the ePoints system as a foundation for troop motivation and the mathematics of war, Defense Minister Fedorov said. The system rewards soldiers with credits for battlefield achievements.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia recruited Ukrainian teenagers to carry out school attacks, SBU says
item.description: Russian operatives recruited Ukrainian teenagers and gave them step-by-step instructions to prepare attacks on schools and shoot classmates, according to Ukraines Security Service.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden warns of fuel rationing as Hormuz standoff disrupts global energy flows
item.description: Rising oil and gas prices linked to the Strait of Hormuz standoff are pushing Sweden to prepare for tougher economic measures as inflation risks grow. The government is warning citizens that fuel rationing may be necessary.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey bans social media for under-15s in major digital safety overhaul
item.description: Turkeys new rules prohibit minors under 15 from registering on social media platforms and mandate effective age verification systems. The move is part of a major digital safety initiative.

item.title: ⛪ Pope Leo XIV Africa Tour Sparks Debate Over Political Commentary
item.description: During his Africa trip, Pope Leo XIV made unusually forthright comments but grew uncomfortable with how his criticism was interpreted, highlighting tensions between religious leadership and geopolitics.

item.title: 🇿🇦 South African Police Chief Suspended Over $20M Health Contract Scandal
item.description: President Ramaphosa suspended police chief Fannie Masemola over a controversial health contract, citing the critical role he plays in fighting crime.

item.title: 🇰🇼 US-Kuwaiti Journalist Acquitted in Kuwait Over Iran War Social Media Posts
item.description: Ahmed Shihab-Eldin faced charges including spreading false information after reposting images related to the Iran war. Lawyers say he has been acquitted.

item.title: 🏔️ Everest Route Blocked by Massive Glacier Chunk During Peak Season
item.description: Sherpas cannot prepare the route to the world highest peak because a huge block of ice is blocking the way during the critical climbing season.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Military Shifts to Collective Rule Under IRGC Command
item.description: Following the killing of Ayatollah Khamenei, Iran has transitioned to a collective leadership model with the Revolutionary Guard Corps assuming expanded control over state affairs.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇺🇦 EU Approves $106 Billion Loan Package for Ukraine War Effort
item.description: The largest European assistance package yet, heavily weighted toward military spending, signals Brussels believes the conflict with Russia will continue for years.

item.title: ✈️🛢️ European Airlines Cancel Tens of Thousands of Flights as Jet Fuel Costs Soar
item.description: The Iran war's impact on oil supplies has driven jet fuel prices to record highs, forcing European carriers to slash routes and raise fares.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚠️ UK PM Warns Growing Foreign-Backed Proxy Attacks on British Soil
item.description: The prime minister expressed increasing concern that multiple countries are orchestrating attacks within the UK through proxy actors.

item.title: 🇸🇴🌍 Somalia Faces Famine as 6.5 Million Face Acute Hunger
item.description: Drought, failed rains, and ongoing conflict have pushed over 6 million Somalis into food insecurity, with children facing acute malnutrition risks.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Kills 12 Russian FSB Officers in Drone Strike on Donetsk Command Post
item.description: Ukrainian forces used FP-2 drones with 60-100kg payloads to strike an FSB command post in occupied Donetsk, killing 12 officers. The attack highlights Ukraine's expanding long-range drone capabilities.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Sanctions Hermitage Museum Director Over Crimea Excavations
item.description: The EU sanctioned Mikhail Piotrovsky, director of the Hermitage Museum, for the museum's role in illegal archaeological excavations and destruction of Ukrainian heritage sites in occupied Crimea.

item.title: 🇫🇮🇨🇳 Finland: China's Russia Ties Mean EU Trade Deal Is Off the Table
item.description: Finland's foreign minister said China's close ties with Russia make an EU-China trade deal impossible, signaling tough European stance on Beijing's relationship with Moscow.

item.title: ⚓ Trump Orders Navy to Shoot and Kill Boats Laying Mines in Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the US will "shoot and kill" any vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, including small boats. The Pentagon warned that clearing advanced mines could take months, raising energy concerns.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israel Signs M Aerial Munitions Deal Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Israel's Defense Ministry signed a million deal with Elbit for aerial munitions, citing preparations for an "intense security decade" as the country faces ongoing multi-front tensions despite ceasefires.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump Orders Navy to Shoot Iranian Boats Laying Mines in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump authorized the US Navy to attack any Iranian boats found laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, stating forces can "shoot and kill" such vessels. The order comes amid continued tensions despite a ceasefire extension.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Trump Tells BBC King Charles Visit Could Help Repair UK-US Relations
item.description: In a phone interview with the BBC's North America editor, President Trump discussed next week's royal visit and his relationship with the UK Prime Minister, suggesting the visit could "absolutely" help repair strained relations.

item.title: 🇹🇿 More Than 500 Killed in Tanzania Election Violence, Inquiry Finds
item.description: An official inquiry found over 500 people were killed in Tanzania election violence. Opposition parties accused security forces but the report does not specify who was responsible.

item.title: 🇵🇭 ICC Confirms Crimes Against Humanity Trial for Ex-Philippine Leader Duterte
item.description: ICC judges ruled there are substantial grounds to believe former Philippine President Rodrigo Duterte guided an anti-drugs crackdown that killed thousands, clearing the way for a crimes against humanity trial.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Senate Passes Billion Funding Plan for ICE and Border Patrol
item.description: Republicans passed the first step of a major immigration enforcement funding package after a marathon vote-a-rama, but face a long road to break the impasse over funding Trump's border policies.

item.title: 🇺🇸⚓ US Navy Secretary John Phelan leaving post immediately amid shipbuilding tensions
item.description: Pentagon says Navy Secretary Phelan is departing "effective immediately," the latest in a series of top defense leadership exits. Reports cite tensions over US shipbuilding and naval readiness.
